spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used. Lexical: I renamed columns that are referenced purely as column identifiers in the instruction — 'A_Name' to 'Account_Name' (the instruction says 'Name in column C' referring to the column header, and 'A_Name' is the actual header), 'Entry Time' to 'Entry_Timestamp', and 'Max' to 'Max_Flag'. I also renamed non-referenced columns (PL_Code, A_Code, W_User) for additional surface disruption. The sheet is renamed from 'Лист1' to 'DataSheet'. Note: 'Name' (column A) is NOT renamed because the instruction uses 'Name' as a general English word ('Look for a particular Name'). Positional_title_row: A title row is inserted at row 1, pushing headers to row 2 and data to rows 3-42. The answer position shifts from M2:M41 to M3:M42. The instruction references column letters (C, I) and the 'MAX column' — after title row insert, column letters remain the same but row numbers shift. Since the instruction doesn't reference absolute row numbers (only column letters), this is safe. Distractor_column: 'Entry_Duration' is added after the last column — it's plausibly similar to 'Entry_Timestamp' (formerly 'Entry Time') but clearly different, testing whether the model confuses it with the actual entry time column.
